content:
APS Score Required for Accounting 2026

Studying BCom Accounting is one of the most popular university programmes in South Africa, leading to careers as a Chartered Accountant (CA), accountant, auditor, or financial manager. This guide covers the APS score required for Accounting programmes at all major South African universities in 2026.

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score and see which universities you qualify for.

APS Requirements for BCom Accounting at SA Universities (2026)

UniversityMin APS for AccountingMathematics RequirementEnglish Requirement
University of Cape Town (UCT)~36 (UCT composite score)Mathematics 6+ (70%+)English 6+ (70%+)
Stellenbosch University (SU)36+Mathematics 6+ (70%+)English 5+ (60%+)
University of Pretoria (UP)28+Mathematics 5+ (60%+)English 4+ (50%+)
University of the Witwatersrand (Wits)28+ (BCom)Mathematics 5+ (60%+)English 4+ (50%+)
University of Johannesburg (UJ)26+Mathematics 4+ (50%+)English 4+ (50%+)
UKZN26+Mathematics 4+ (50%+)English 4+ (50%+)
Rhodes University26+Mathematics 5+ (60%+)English 5+ (60%+)
University of the Free State (UFS)26+Mathematics 4+ (50%+)English 4+ (50%+)
Nelson Mandela University (NMU)24+Mathematics 4+ (50%+)English 4+ (50%+)
University of the Western Cape (UWC)22+Mathematics 4+ (50%+)English 4+ (50%+)
Walter Sisulu University (WSU)20+Mathematics 3+ (40%+)English 3+ (40%+)
University of Limpopo22+Mathematics 3+ (40%+)English 4+ (50%+)
UNISA (distance learning)No APS — subject requirements onlyMathematics 3+ (40%+)English 3+ (40%+)

Note: APS requirements can change annually. Always verify the latest requirements on the university's official website before applying.

What Subjects Do You Need for Accounting?

While requirements vary by university, the typical subject requirements for BCom Accounting are:

  • Mathematics: Pure Mathematics is required or strongly preferred at most universities. Mathematical Literacy may be accepted at some institutions (WSU, UNISA) but not at UCT, Stellenbosch, Wits, or UP
  • English: English Home Language or First Additional Language at Level 4–6 depending on the university
  • Accounting: Not usually a formal requirement, but highly recommended as it provides a strong foundation
  • Business Studies / Economics: Helpful but not usually required

Does Accounting at Matric Help for BCom Accounting?

While Accounting (Grade 12) is not a prerequisite at most universities, it is highly recommended. Students who studied Accounting in Grade 10–12 tend to find first-year university accounting much more manageable. If you're serious about a BCom Accounting degree, study Accounting through matric.

BCom Accounting vs. Other Accounting Degrees

QualificationDurationCareer PathTypical Min APS
BCom Accounting3 yearsCA(SA), accountant, financial manager22–36+
BCom Financial Management3 yearsFinancial manager, analyst20–30+
BCom Management Accounting3 yearsManagement accountant, CIMA route20–28+
National Diploma in Accounting (TVET/UoT)3 yearsBookkeeper, accounts clerk15–20+
B.Tech Accounting (UoT)4 years totalTechnical accountant20–24+

Pathway to CA(SA) Through BCom Accounting

The Chartered Accountant (CA(SA)) designation is the most respected accounting qualification in South Africa. To become a CA(SA):

  1. BCom Accounting at a SAICA-accredited university (3 years)
  2. Honours/CTA (Certificate in the Theory of Accounting) (1 year)
  3. SAICA Initial Test of Competence (ITC) — written exam
  4. 3-year trainee accountant contract (articles) at an accredited training office
  5. Assessment of Professional Competence (APC) — final qualifying exam

Universities with SAICA accreditation for BCom Accounting include: UCT, Wits, UP, Stellenbosch, UKZN, UJ, Rhodes, UFS, NMU, UWC, and others.

How to Improve Your Accounting APS

If your APS is below the requirements for your target university, focus on:

  • Mathematics: This is the single most important subject for accounting admissions. Aim for 60%+ (Level 5) at minimum
  • English: Strong language skills are essential for university-level studies
  • Accounting (if you're taking it): Past papers practice is key

Struggling with Mathematics or Accounting in Grade 10–12? StudyBuddy's AI tutor provides personalised help in all CAPS subjects — most students improve 15–25% within weeks. Start free today.
keywords: APS requirements for accounting at UCT
APS requirements for accounting at Wits
quickAnswer: The APS score required for BCom Accounting varies by university: UCT needs ~36, Stellenbosch 36+, Wits 28+, UP 28+, UJ 26+, UWC 22+, and WSU 20+. Most universities require Pure Mathematics (not Mathematical Literacy). Use the free APS Calculator at /aps-calculator.

faqs.question: What APS do I need for BCom Accounting in South Africa?
faqs.answer: APS requirements for BCom Accounting vary by university: top universities like UCT and Stellenbosch require 36+, while mid-range options like Wits and UP need 28+, and more accessible options like UWC require 22+ and WSU 20+. UNISA has no APS requirement but requires specific subjects.

faqs.question: Can I study BCom Accounting with Mathematical Literacy?
faqs.answer: Most traditional universities (UCT, Wits, UP, Stellenbosch, UKZN) do not accept Mathematical Literacy for BCom Accounting — they require Pure Mathematics. Some institutions like WSU and UNISA may accept Mathematical Literacy. Always check the specific requirements for your target institution.

faqs.question: Do you need Accounting as a subject to study BCom Accounting?
faqs.answer: Most universities do not require Grade 12 Accounting as a prerequisite for BCom Accounting. However, studying Accounting in Grade 10–12 is highly recommended as it provides a strong foundation and makes first-year university accounting much easier.

faqs.question: What is the easiest university to get into for accounting in South Africa?
faqs.answer: UNISA (University of South Africa) has no APS requirement and only requires specific subject passes — making it the most accessible option for BCom Accounting. For contact learning, WSU requires APS 20+ and UWC requires 22+. UNISA is ideal for working students as it offers distance learning.
